prompt: Summarize the key obligations, term, termination triggers, and liability cap in this SaaS Master Services Agreement: 'Vendor shall provide the Hosted Service with 99.9% uptime measured monthly excluding scheduled maintenance. Term is 36 months from Effective Date, auto-renewing for 12-month periods unless either party gives 60 days written notice. Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 30 days written notice. Vendor's total liability is capped at fees paid in the trailing 12 months, excluding indemnity for IP infringement and breach of confidentiality.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability cap: 'Customer pays $48,000 annually in advance. The Service is provided 'as is'; Vendor disclaims all implied warranties. Term: 24 months, no auto-renew. Either party may terminate for convenience with 90 days notice after month 12. Vendor's liability is capped at 6 months fees. Force majeure events suspend obligations for up to 60 days, after which either party may terminate.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, liability cap: 'Provider grants Customer a non-exclusive license to the Platform during the Subscription Term (initial 12 months, auto-renews monthly). Customer may terminate any auto-renewal with 15 days notice. Provider may terminate immediately for non-payment after 10 days written demand. Liability cap: greater of (a) 12 months fees or (b) $100,000. Carve-outs: indemnification, breach of Section 9 (Confidentiality), and gross negligence.'

Summarize the recipient's obligations, term, and survival clauses: 'Recipient shall hold Confidential Information in strict confidence for a period of 5 years from disclosure, using at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information, and shall not disclose it to any third party except employees with a need-to-know who are bound by equivalent obligations. Obligations survive termination of the underlying business relationship.'

Summarize obligations, term, and survival: 'Receiving Party agrees to: (i) protect Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using reasonable measures; (ii) limit access to personnel with a legitimate need; (iii) not use such information for any purpose outside this engagement. Confidentiality obligations survive for 3 years post-termination, except trade secrets which survive indefinitely.'

Summarize obligations and survival: 'Each party (as Recipient) shall protect the other party's (as Discloser) Confidential Information for a period of 7 years following termination of this Agreement. Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that is or becomes publicly known through no fault of Recipient, was rightfully known prior to disclosure, or is independently developed.'

Summarize the employee's key obligations, IP assignment, non-compete, and termination clauses: 'Employee assigns to Company all Inventions conceived during employment that relate to Company's business or are made using Company resources. Employee shall not, for 12 months post-termination, solicit Company's customers within North America. Either party may terminate at will with 2 weeks notice. Severance: 4 weeks pay if terminated without cause.'

Summarize obligations, IP, non-compete, termination: 'All work product created within scope of employment is work-for-hire and owned by Employer. Employee shall not engage in competing business activity during employment. Employer may terminate immediately for cause (defined as: fraud, gross negligence, material breach of policy). At-will employment otherwise. No severance unless terminated as part of a reduction in force.'

Summarize obligations, IP, non-compete, termination: 'Employee hereby assigns all right, title and interest in Inventions, whether patentable or not, conceived during the term of employment that (a) relate to the Company's business, or (b) result from work performed for Company. Non-solicitation period: 18 months. Employee may not solicit Company employees or contractors. Termination requires 30 days notice from either party.'

Summarize the IP licensing terms, royalty, and termination triggers: 'Licensor grants Licensee a worldwide, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Patented Technology in Field of Use (defined as: industrial water treatment, excluding municipal). Royalty: 4% of Net Sales, paid quarterly, with $50,000 annual minimum. Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 60 days. Licensor may terminate immediately upon Licensee's bankruptcy or assignment for benefit of creditors.'
